title: Commodities Brief — 03 Jul 2026
intro:
Precious metals surge across the board with gold breaching $4190 and silver rallying over 8% this settlement, signaling robust safe-haven demand amid apparent geopolitical or monetary policy concerns. Energy markets remain subdued with WTI holding below $69 despite inventory dynamics, while agricultural commodities show strength led by corn's 5.7% weekly advance on weather-related supply anxieties.
🥇 PRECIOUS METALS
Gold $4192.30 +1.94% | Bias: LONG | Key: $4150 support / $4250 resistance. New all-time highs suggest sustained institutional inflows and potential dollar weakness. Silver $63.10 +4.05% outperforming with 2.1x gold's gains, confirming industrial-monetary dual demand. Platinum $1670.50 +3.33% benefits from automotive sector recovery and supply constraints from South African producers. PGM complex showing coordinated strength with palladium up 6.25% weekly. Gold-silver ratio compressing to 66.4, indicating silver catch-up momentum likely to continue.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES SUMMARY
sections.content:
Precious metals surge across the board with gold breaching $4190 and silver rallying over 8% this settlement, signaling robust safe-haven demand amid apparent geopolitical or monetary policy concerns. Energy markets remain subdued with WTI holding below $69 despite inventory dynamics, while agricultural commodities show strength led by corn's 5.7% weekly advance on weather-related supply anxieties.
🥇 PRECIOUS METALS
Gold $4192.30 +1.94% | Bias: LONG | Key: $4150 support / $4250 resistance. New all-time highs suggest sustained institutional inflows and potential dollar weakness. Silver $63.10 +4.05% outperforming with 2.1x gold's gains, confirming industrial-monetary dual demand. Platinum $1670.50 +3.33% benefits from automotive sector recovery and supply constraints from South African producers. PGM complex showing coordinated strength with palladium up 6.25% weekly. Gold-silver ratio compressing to 66.4, indicating silver catch-up momentum likely to continue.

sections.heading: ⚡ ENERGY
sections.content:
WTI $68.78 +0.13% | Bias: NEUTRAL-TO-WEAK | Key: $66.50 / $71.00. Brent $72.09 +0.40% maintaining modest premium with Brent-WTI spread at $3.31, reflecting persistent Cushing inventory overhangs versus tighter Brent fundamentals. Crude weakness despite metals strength suggests demand concerns outweighing geopolitical risk premiums. Natural Gas $3.25 +1.78% showing seasonal support ahead of summer cooling demand, but gains remain modest. Energy complex detached from broader commodity rally indicates sector-specific headwinds or recessionary demand fears.
🌾 AGRICULTURE
Corn $425.00 +0.95% and up 5.72% this week on Midwest heat forecasts threatening pollination during critical growth stage. Wheat $590.50 -0.25% consolidating after 3.69% weekly gain on Black Sea export disruption concerns. Soybeans $1131.75 +0.49% supported by South American crop downgrades and Chinese purchasing activity resuming.

sections.heading: 🎯 TRADE IDEAS
sections.content:
LONG: Silver entry $62.80 — outperformance continuation, GSR compression — SL $60.50 — TP $68.00
LONG: Corn entry $424.00 — weather premium expansion, technical breakout — SL $415.00 — TP $445.00
SHORT: WTI entry $69.20 — demand weakness, resistance rejection — SL $71.50 — TP $65.50

sections.heading: ⚠️ RISKS
sections.content:
Abrupt Fed pivot or geopolitical de-escalation could trigger sharp precious metals profit-taking from extended levels.
Chinese economic data disappoint next week may cascade across industrial metals and energy, reversing agricultural demand assumptions.
